title: Neohouzeaua dullooa
intro: Neohouzeaua dullooa, also known as Dullooa tree or Himalayan tree, is a species of evergreen tree belonging to the family Pinaceae. The wood of this plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing the risk of chronic diseases like cancer and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Neohouzeaua dullooa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Neohouzeaua dullooa is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder to utilize its therapeutic benefits, commonly used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Neohouzeaua dullooa is a species of tree in the family Pinaceae. It is native to the Himalayas and is commonly found in the mountainous regions of Asia. The classification of Neohouzeaua dullooa is as follows: it belongs to the genus Neohouzeaua, which is part of the subfamily Laricoideae within the family Pinaceae. Neohouzeaua dullooa is often grouped with other conifers in the Pinaceae family due to its shared characteristics and physical traits.
